summary: Large Ice Mountain stage decompilation pass centered on src/melee/gr/gricemt.c. The PR converted many placeholder or partial routines into C, corrected Ice Mountain ground variable layouts and function prototypes, moved material/object arrays to the icemt2 union fields where appropriate, and updated symbols after fn_801F9150 absorbed the former 4-byte fn_801F91A4 slot. decomp.dev reported +0.10% matched code / +3844 bytes, 13 new full matches in gricemt, and 11 additional improvements, with one intentionally broken/removed tiny match for fn_801F91A4.
what_changed: Implemented or substantially improved many gricemt routines, including fn_801F8E58, fn_801F9150, fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448, fn_801F9558, grIceMt_801F993C, grIceMt_801F9ACC, grIceMt_801FA0BC, grIceMt_801FA500, and grIceMt_801FA7F0.
Completed several Ice Mountain row/setup callbacks such as grIceMt_801F7D94, grIceMt_801F7F70, grIceMt_801F8208, grIceMt_801F83EC, grIceMt_801F865C, grIceMt_801F8850, and grIceMt_801F8B10.
Corrected grIceMt_GroundVars and grIceMt_GroundVars2 layouts in types.h with explicit gp-relative offsets, padding, x100/x108 s16 arrays, and moved xF8 HSD_GObj storage into grIceMt_GroundVars2.

Updated gricemt.h prototypes: IceMountainParams xB0/xB4 became s16 pointers, fn_801F8E58/fn_801F9150 now take output pointers and return s32, grIceMt_801F96E0 now accepts struct grIceMt_GroundVars*, grIceMt_801FA0BC returns void, and grIceMt_801FA500 now takes an HSD_JObj target.
Replaced many raw __assert calls with HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG and added baselib/debug.h and baselib/forward.h includes.
Added local/extern data helpers such as GrIm825C, GrIm588, grIm_803E4068 initialized row data, and extern sdata constants around grIm_804DB58C/grIm_804DB590/etc.
Changed many mistaken gp->gv.corneria and gp->gv.icemt references to the correct gp->gv.icemt or gp->gv.icemt2 fields.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so fn_801F9150 size is 0x58 and removed separate fn_801F91A4 symbol.
smart_moves: The struct-layout work appears central: correcting grIceMt_GroundVars offsets and separating runtime HSD_GObj arrays into grIceMt_GroundVars2 unlocked many downstream matches and avoided false field accesses through unrelated unions.
Using tiny local stack structs such as GrIm588, GrIm825C, and anonymous two-u32 structs helped reproduce stack copies from rodata/sdata into s16 joint-index lists passed to grIceMt_801F8CDC.
The PR reused HSD_ASSERT and HSD_ASSERTMSG with source line constants instead of hand-written __assert blocks, reducing code noise and preserving matching assert line-number behavior.
Introducing PAD_STACK in several routines was a targeted matching tactic for stack frame shape without inventing fake logic.
The helper inline wrappers around grIceMt_801F71E8 in grIceMt_801F9ACC/grIceMt_801FA0BC suggest a deliberate inlining/noinline control tactic to match codegen around repeated ground object lookup.
Replacing small sdata float globals with direct literals like 0.0f, -1.0f, 1.0f, and 6.0f where the compiler expected immediates improved rodata/sdata placement and matching.
decomp_lessons: For stage files with multiple per-stage ground variable unions, verify the active union member before chasing mismatches; many fixes here were icemt versus icemt2 field corrections.
When a nominal function is only a 4-byte stub between two routines, later decompilation may prove it is not a real separate function; keep symbols.txt aligned with actual function boundaries.
Header prototypes can block matches: callbacks that fill an out parameter and return an id should be typed that way, not left as UNK_RET or unrelated pointer returns.
Data typing matters: IceMtRowData x4/x8 needed to be f32 rather than u32 to model row spacing/translation calculations and match floating-point code.
For joint-index lists passed as s16*, copying small const structs into stack temporaries can better reflect original code than manually constructing arrays.

Object lifecycle routines should clear or destroy existing Ground_801C2BA4 objects when segment ids are not -1, then reset flags; this pattern was used in grIceMt_801F796C and segment scrolling routines.
Collision callbacks can be matched by first reconstructing their flag-gated animation side effects, then delegating to a shared helper like grIceMt_801FA7F0.
naming_conventions: Stage globals use the grIm_ prefix for Ice Mountain data, e.g. grIm_803E4068, grIm_804D69F4, grIm_804DB588.
Functions retain address-based names in the grIceMt_801Fxxxx or fn_801Fxxxx style where semantic names are not known.
Temporary object variables were improved from generic gobj to bg_gobj or mgobj to reflect background/managed ground object roles.
Unknown struct fields continue to use x-offset names, but the updated comments in types.h record gp-relative offsets such as +00 gp+C4 and +30 gp+F4.
The PR distinguishes Ice Mountain ground variable structs as grIceMt_GroundVars and grIceMt_GroundVars2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x10), PAD_STACK(8), and PAD_STACK(4) in several functions to match expected stack layouts.
Used HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTMSG macros with exact line constants instead of open-coded __assert calls.
Used casts to callback types, including HSD_GObjEvent and mpLib_Callback, to satisfy call signatures while preserving generated code.
Used direct pointer arithmetic/casts where necessary, such as (s16*) ((u8*) gp + 0x100), to match accesses before the structure layout was fully semantic.
Added inline wrapper chains around grIceMt_801F71E8 to influence call/inlining behavior.
Converted double-looking placeholders to explicit f32 math in grIceMt_801F8B10 and segment distance calculations.
Adjusted symbols.txt function boundaries after fn_801F9150 size changed from 0x54 to 0x58 and fn_801F91A4 disappeared as an independent symbol.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.

decomp.dev reported 13 new matches in main/melee/gr/gricemt: fn_801F9338, fn_801F9448, fn_801F9558, grIceMt_801F7F70, grIceMt_801F83EC, grIceMt_801F8B10, grIceMt_801F796C, fn_801F9150, grIceMt_801F865C, grIceMt_801FA7F0, grIceMt_801F8208, grIceMt_801F8850, and grIceMt_801F7D94.
decomp.dev also reported one broken match: fn_801F91A4 dropped from 100% to 0%, consistent with the symbol map removing it and extending fn_801F9150.

decomp.dev reported major unmatched improvements for grIceMt_801F9ACC, grIceMt_801FA0BC, fn_801F8E58, grIceMt_801F993C, grIceMt_801FA500, .data, .rodata, grIceMt_801F96E0, .sdata2, grIceMt_801F686C, and .sdata.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type grIceMt_801F9ACC, grIceMt_801FA0BC, and grIceMt_801FA6D8.
Check whether the grIceMt_GroundVars x100/x108 arrays later receive semantic names for collision/animation state.
Search for other stage PRs that remove tiny fn_* symbols after correcting function boundaries.
Look for follow-up work on grIm_804DB58C/grIm_804DB590/grIm_804DB594 and related sdata constants.
Compare Ice Mountain segment scrolling code against other vertical-scrolling stage implementations for reusable field names.
